---
title: "NO 3D with geforce4 mx and nvidia-glx drivers"
date: 2008-06-12
forum: Multimedia Software
---

### Post by BennyLZ on 2008-06-12
Hi.
I own an nvidia geforce4 mx integrated gpu, I have the official nvidia-glx drivers activated, but when I start a 3D application (for example gnome 3D chess, open-arena or enemy territory) I hear the sound but I can't see the video (with or without compiz).
I normally don't use compiz, but if I active it and use the cube 3d, it works!
here there's my xorg.conf:
```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Tue Jan 22 19:53:46 PST 2008
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

### Post by Devastatorius on 2008-06-13
> **BennyLZ said:**
> no one can help me? :(
please, I need 3D! I don't want to come back to windows!

Try install nvidia-glx-legacy driver if you have geforce4 mx series video card.

---

### Post by BennyLZ on 2008-06-13
> **Devastatorius said:**
> Try install nvidia-glx-legacy driver if you have geforce4 mx series video card.

nvidia-glx-legacy is for video cards older than geforce3. Anyway I tried to install it, but it didn't work (I had only the command line, with no desktop)... so I returned back to nvidia-glx. :confused:

---

### Post by xc3RnbFO8P on 2008-06-13
You can try to change:
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"[COLOR="Red"]Enable[/COLOR]"
EndSection
to
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"[COLOR="SeaGreen"]Disable[/COLOR]"
EndSection

---

### Post by Devastatorius on 2008-06-13
> **BennyLZ said:**
> nvidia-glx-legacy is for video cards older than geforce3. Anyway I tried to install it, but it didn't work (I had only the command line, with no desktop)... so I returned back to nvidia-glx. :confused:
Well, i have old computer in my work with geforce4 mx440 card.
For me 3D working only with legacy driver. So, i installed nvidia-glx-legacy driver, restarted PC, and in the console used command:
```
dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```And restarted computer again. When it boots in graphical mode, i installed nvidia-settings:
```
sudo apt-get install nvidia-settings
```Launched as root:
```
sudo nvidia-settings
```Set resolution, monitor refresh rate at last i clicked Apply and Save to X Configuration File buttons, reboot PC, when it boots, i have full 3D mode :)

---

ok, I installed legacy driver and reboot.
then I wrote: ```
dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```, I finished the wizard, and reboot. nothing happened.
I think I didn't understand well what I have to do in the wizard... it configured the keyboard but not the video :confused:

EDIT: red that in hardy dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg doesn't configure the video...
